# Envelope Encryption

Transit facilitates the use of Envelope Encryption, a pattern where Transit
can produce sets of Data Encryption Keys (DEKs) and Encrypted Data Keys
(EDKs), which a client side library can use to encrypt information client
side, embedding the EDK into the result.  At decryption time, the client
can reach out to Vault with the EDK and ask to decrypt it to the DEK, then
use the DEK to decrypt the data.

## Setup and Permissions

The data key set endpoints are applicable to Transit symmetric encryption
keys.  To use Envelope Encryption,
[create](/vault/api-docs/secret/transit#create-key) a symmetric encryption
key, ideally `aes256-gcm96` or `chacha20-poly1305`.  

Callers that want to perform client side envelope encryption will need the
following ACL:

```hcl
path "_transit-mount_/_encryption-key_/datakeys" {  
  capabilities = ["create", "update"]
}

path "_transit-mount_/_encryption-key_/derived_keys" {  
  capabilities = ["create", "update"]
}

```

For decryption:

```hcl
path "_transit-mount_/_encryption-key_/decrypt" {  
  capabilities = ["create", "update"]
}
```

## SDK

A Go [SDK](https://github.com/hashicorp/vault-envelope-encryption-sdk) is
provided that handles key fetching from Transit and provides functions for
encrypting and decrypting from streams.  This makes it trivial for clients
to encrypt and decrypt files and streams in application logic.

## Command Line

The Vault CLI includes commands for using the data key set endpoints to
easily [encrypt](/vault/docs/commands/transit/envelope_encrypt) and 
[decrypt](/vault/docs/commands/transit/envelope_decrypt) files and streams locally.
